result:
The plan is fully implemented and verified. The remaining untracked items are HubLaunch's own runtime directories (jobs/logs/lessons), not part of this plan. ``` [RALPH] ✅ MISSION COMPLETE ``` **Summary:** - **Implemented:** - `index.html` — semantic HTML5 page: `

Welcome to Hula

`, under-construction `
` with 🚧 icon, "Under Construction" heading, and "We're working hard to bring you something great. Check back soon.", title `Hula — Coming Soon`, viewport meta, `lang="en"`, the required HTML comment. - `styles.css` — CSS reset, centered flex layout, amber under-construction banner, responsive relative units. Self-contained (no external resources). - `.github/workflows/pages.yml` — deploys the repo root of `main` to GitHub Pages (no existing Pages workflow was found). - **Verification (all passed):** - AC1–AC7 checked programmatically: file exists, correct `

`, under-construction notice with icon/heading/message, correct ``, viewport meta present, **no `<script>`/JavaScript**, **no external resource links** (verified in both HTML and CSS). - HTML tag balance validated (no unclosed/misnested tags). - 🚧 emoji confirmed UTF-8 encoded with `charset="UTF-8"` declared. - No type-check/lint/build tooling exists for this plain static project (no `package.json`), so there was nothing further to run — confirmed rather than assumed. - **Notes / blocker (requires a human):** Per the plan and AC8, GitHub Pages must be manually enabled once in **Settings → Pages → Source → "GitHub Actions"** by a repo admin. This cannot be done from code — the workflow will only deploy after that toggle. Recorded in the lessons file.
